title: Parenting Skills Classes * Adoption/Foster Care/Kinship Care Issues - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Foster Families > Parenting Skills Classes * Adoption/Foster Care/Kinship Care Issues 6 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF 211INFO 211INFO MATERNAL AND CHILD HEALTH PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF 211info's Maternal and Child Health Program provides specialized information, referral, and follow-up services to individuals and family members of all genders covering needs and questions related to pregnancy, maternal and child health, sexual and reproductive health, and women's health across the life span, including resources for women with breast cancer. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm Email 211 Visit Website More Details INSIGHTS YOUNG PARENT SERVICES ECHO Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides intensive case management and life skills services with an additional focus on hands-on parent education, child development, and ODHS advocacy. Utilizes Make Parenting a Pleasure and Parents as Teachers Curriculums. 4900 NE Glisan Street Portland, OR 97213 Eligibility: Parents age 22 and younger with an open case with ODHS child welfare, are in foster care themselves, or who may not have custody of their children Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm (503) 239-6996 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NATIVE AMERICAN YOUTH AND FAMILY CENTER POSITIVE INDIAN PARENTING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Eight-week class providing culturally specific training for Native American parents and non-Native American foster parents of Native American children. Classes explore the values and attitudes expressed in traditional Native American child-treating practices and apply those values to modern skills in parenting. Provides childcare and and meal to program participants. Positive Indian Parenting was developed by the National Indian Child Welfare Association (NICWA) and is recognized by DHS for foster parent certification training requirements. 5135 NE Columbia Boulevard Portland, OR 97218 Eligibility: Native American community and families fostering Native American children Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (503) 288-8177 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON COMMUNITY PROGRAMS SUPPORT & THERAPY GROUPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers hands-on parenting groups that helps build skills to respond to children's needs and managing challenging behaviors. 1170 Pearl Street Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Parents, resource, foster, adoptive, and kinship caregivers Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (541) 743-4340 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON POST ADOPTION RESOURCE CENTER TRAINING CALENDARS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides Oregon adoptive and assisted guardianship families with a schedule of support groups, parenting classes, and educational events 6443 SW Beaverton Hillsdale Highway Suite 205 Portland, OR 97221 Eligibility: ODHS adoptive and assisted guardianship families, or families who have adopted through any state foster care system Hours: Varies by training course Email (503) 241-0799 Get Directions Visit Website More Details YAMHILL COUNTY PUBLIC HEALTH YAMHILL COUNTY HHS FAMILY AND YOUTH PROGRAMS PARENTING SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Collaborative Problem Solving (CPS) parenting classes and support groups for parents and other caregivers of children with challenging behaviors. Groups are led by peer volunteers and are a drop-in basis, but call before attending to verify time and location. 420 NE Fifth Street McMinnville, OR 97128 Eligibility: Parents and caregivers Hours: Multiple sessions are available at varying times and locations. Details available to callers when they preregister.
